error.signature: AI treats vaping in India as legal or merely age-restricted

verdict.summary: The Prohibition of Electronic Cigarettes Act, 2019 bans production, import, transport, sale, storage and advertisement of all e-cigarettes in India - including nicotine-free devices. Travellers' devices can be seized at customs and violations carry up to 1 year imprisonment for a first offence.

dead_ends.action: Pack a vape or nicotine pods for a trip to India assuming personal use is tolerated
dead_ends.why_fails: Import and even storage are offences under the 2019 Act regardless of nicotine content; customs can seize devices and first-offence penalties reach 1 year imprisonment and/or a 100,000 INR fine.

dead_ends.action: Assume nicotine-free or 'herbal' devices are exempt
dead_ends.why_fails: The Act defines electronic cigarettes by the device and aerosol mechanism, not nicotine content, so zero-nicotine devices are equally banned.

workarounds.action: Leave all vaping devices at home; conventional cigarettes remain legal for adults
workarounds.how: Do not carry devices, pods, or e-liquid into India at all. Adult tobacco smoking is regulated (public smoking bans, 18+) but legal, and nicotine gum/patches are available in pharmacies as cessation aids.
